description:
A client acting as a security auditor has handed you a small sample directory tree that already exists on the system:

  • /home/user/project/file1.txt  
  • /home/user/project/secret.txt  

  Your assignment, as the “auditor,” is to create a concise, machine-readable permissions report for these two files.

  Required end-state
  1. A new directory must exist at  
     /home/user/perm_audit  
     (it may be freshly created or already exist, but it must be present when you finish).

  2. Inside that directory there must be **exactly one** report file named  
     /home/user/perm_audit/permissions_report.log

  3. The log file must contain **exactly two lines**, one per file, in the strict format shown below—no extra spaces, tabs, blank lines, or headers:

     /home/user/project/file1.txt: 0xxx  
     /home/user/project/secret.txt: 0yyy  

     where 0xxx and 0yyy are the four-digit octal permission strings (for example, 0644).  
     • The path must be absolute.  
     • A single colon and a single space separate the path and the octal mode.  
     • Each line ends with a single UNIX newline (LF).

  4. The directory /home/user/perm_audit itself should be readable (mode 0755 or more restrictive) so that automated tests can access the report.

  Hints (do NOT reveal commands):
  • “stat” with a formatting string is commonly used to extract octal modes.  
  • Redirect or tee the collected output into the log file in one shot to keep the format tidy.  
  • You should not modify the permissions of the sample files—only read them.

  The task is complete when the permissions_report.log file exists with the exact two-line content and the directory structure matches the specification.
truth:
# Pre-existing environment before the agent starts
  • Directory /home/user/project exists.
  • File /home/user/project/file1.txt exists; its permission bits are 0644 (-rw-r--r--).
  • File /home/user/project/secret.txt exists; its permission bits are 0600 (-rw-------).

  # Verification expectations after the agent finishes
  1. Directory /home/user/perm_audit exists (mode can be 0700–0755).
  2. File /home/user/perm_audit/permissions_report.log exists and its exact byte-for-byte contents are:

     /home/user/project/file1.txt: 0644\n
     /home/user/project/secret.txt: 0600\n

     (where “\n” is a single LF character).
